mission.title: n8n: Incorrect MIME Type when reading file using “Read/Write Files from Disk” node
mission.description: Incorrect MIME Type when reading file using “Read/Write Files from Disk” node. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current n8n deployment
steps.description:
Verify your n8n version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n n8n -l app.kubernetes.io/name=n8n
helm list -n n8n 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working n8n installation.

steps.title: Review n8n configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant n8n configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n n8n -l app.kubernetes.io/name=n8n
kubectl get configmap -n n8n -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=n8n
```
### Bug Description

Hello,,

After updating n8n to the beta version, the `Read/Write Files from Disk` node returns a different MIME type than in the previous version (1.120.3).

steps.title: Apply the fix for Incorrect MIME Type when reading file using “Read/Write…
Upgrade n8n to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade n8n:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade n8n n8n/n8n --namespace n8n
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n n8n
helm list -n n8n
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n n8n -l app.kubernetes.io/name=n8n
kubectl get events -n n8n --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Incorrect MIME Type when reading file using “Read/Write…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
I’ve been running into this issue recently as well.

I’m currently on **n8n 2.1.1**, and what’s confusing is that **some videos upload successfully while others fail with the same workflow**. At first I thought it might be a pure n8n version issue, but around the same time I also changed some **ffmpeg options used to generate the videos**, so that might be part of the trigger too.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with n8n installed or the issue environment reproducible.
